category_title: Elementary students with anger management skill deficits and bullying behaviors
minimal_context: Amy is participating in a group activity with peers during the after-school program.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Amy, please join the other students for the after-school program activity.

STUDENT: They aren't doing what I say. I feel out of control.

TUTOR: Just keep working with your group.

STUDENT: I'm getting angry! I'm going to end up 'getting in trouble' again.

TUTOR: Please try to get along with them.

STUDENT: (Verbally threatens a peer and physically hits another student) Leave me alone!

options.A: Provide feedback pertaining to her responses during check-out at the end of the program (e.g., discuss how seating choices affected performance).
options.B: At the end of each after-school session, have the student turn in her checklist to the tutor and receive an agreed-upon reinforcer.
options.C: Publicly celebrate awards and improvements (like reduced absenteeism) during the after-school program as often as possible throughout the semester.
options.D: Teach and practice self-calming strategies directed by the Second Step curriculum, specifically lessons on 'Managing Strong Feelings' and 'Calming Down Anger'.
